heading: Removal or suspension of management officials
label: 687e Removal or suspension of management officials
notes: References in TextFor definition of “this chapter”, referred to in subsec. (b)(1)(A)(i)(I), (II), see References in Text note set out under .section 661 of this title

Amendments2001— amended section catchline and text generally. Prior to amendment, text related to removal and suspension of directors and officers of licensees, with regard to written notice of intention to remove and grounds for removal; suspension pending completion of administrative proceedings; a hearing upon notice of intention to remove a director or officer and issuance of an order of removal; a stay of suspension and/or prohibition by a United States district court; suspension of directors and officers charged with felonies involving dishonesty or breach of trust; and procedural aspects of hearings provided for in this section.Pub. L. 107–100
amendments: Amendments2001— amended section catchline and text generally. Prior to amendment, text related to removal and suspension of directors and officers of licensees, with regard to written notice of intention to remove and grounds for removal; suspension pending completion of administrative proceedings; a hearing upon notice of intention to remove a director or officer and issuance of an order of removal; a stay of suspension and/or prohibition by a United States district court; suspension of directors and officers charged with felonies involving dishonesty or breach of trust; and procedural aspects of hearings provided for in this section.Pub. L. 107–100

children.heading: Definition of “management official”
children.label: a Definition of “management official”
children.text: In this section, the term “management official” means an officer, director, general partner, manager, employee, agent, or other participant in the management or conduct of the affairs of a licensee.

children.label: I
children.text: this chapter;

children.label: II
children.text: any regulation issued under this chapter; or

children.label: III
children.text: a cease-and-desist order which has become final; or

children.label: ii
children.text: has willfully and knowingly committed or engaged in any act, omission, or practice which constitutes a substantial breach of a fiduciary duty of that person as a management official; and

children.label: B
children.text: the violation or breach of fiduciary duty is one involving personal dishonesty on the part of such management official.

children.heading: Contents of notice
children.label: 2 Contents of notice
children.text: A notice of intention to remove a management official, as provided in paragraph (1), shall contain a statement of the facts constituting grounds therefor, and shall fix a time and place at which a hearing will be held thereon.

children.label: i
children.text: the management official, and for good cause shown; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the Attorney General of the United States.

children.heading: Consent
children.label: B Consent
children.text: Unless the management official shall appear at a hearing described in this paragraph in person or by a duly authorized representative, that management official shall be deemed to have consented to the issuance of an order of removal under paragraph (1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the event of consent under paragraph (3)(B), or if upon the record made at a hearing described in this subsection, the Administrator finds that any of the grounds specified in the notice of removal has been established, the Administrator may issue such orders of removal from office as the Administrator deems appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: become effective at the expiration of 30 days after the date of service upon the subject licensee and the management official concerned (except in the case of an order issued upon consent as described in paragraph (3)(B), which shall become effective at the time specified in such order); and

children.label: ii
children.text: remain effective and enforceable, except to such extent as it is stayed, modified, terminated, or set aside by action of the Administrator or a reviewing court in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Authority to suspend or prohibit participation
children.label: c Authority to suspend or prohibit participation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator may, if the Administrator deems it necessary for the protection of the licensee or the interests of the Administration, suspend from office or prohibit from further participation in any manner in the management or conduct of the affairs of the licensee, or both, any management official referred to in subsection (b)(1), by written notice to such effect served upon the management official.

children.label: A
children.text: shall become effective upon service of notice under paragraph (1); and

children.label: i
children.text: pending the completion of the administrative proceedings pursuant to a notice of intention to remove served under subsection (b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: until such time as the Administrator shall dismiss the charges specified in the notice, or, if an order of removal or prohibition is issued against the management official, until the effective date of any such order.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: 3 Judicial review
children.text: Not later than 10 days after any management official has been suspended from office or prohibited from participation in the management or conduct of the affairs of a licensee, or both, under paragraph (1), that management official may apply to the United States district court for the judicial district in which the home office of the licensee is located, or the United States District Court for the District of Columbia, for a stay of the suspension or prohibition pending the completion of the administrative proceedings pursuant to a notice of intent to remove served upon the management official under subsection (b), and such court shall have jurisdiction to stay such action.

children.heading: Authority to suspend on criminal charges
children.label: d Authority to suspend on criminal charges

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Whenever a management official is charged in any information, indictment, or complaint authorized by a United States attorney, with the commission of or participation in a felony involving dishonesty or breach of trust, the Administrator may, by written notice served upon that management official, suspend that management official from office or prohibit that management official from further participation in any manner in the management or conduct of the affairs of the licensee, or both.

children.heading: Effectiveness
children.label: 2 Effectiveness
children.text: A suspension or prohibition under paragraph (1) shall remain in effect until the subject information, indictment, or complaint is finally disposed of, or until terminated by the Administrator.

children.heading: Authority upon conviction
children.label: 3 Authority upon conviction
children.text: If a judgment of conviction with respect to an offense described in paragraph (1) is entered against a management official, then at such time as the judgment is not subject to further appellate review, the Administrator may issue and serve upon the management official an order removing that management official, which removal shall become effective upon service of a copy of the order upon the licensee.

children.heading: Authority upon dismissal or other disposition
children.label: 4 Authority upon dismissal or other disposition
children.text: A finding of not guilty or other disposition of charges described in paragraph (1) shall not preclude the Administrator from thereafter instituting proceedings to suspend or remove the management official from office, or to prohibit the management official from participation in the management or conduct of the affairs of the licensee, or both, pursuant to subsection (b) or (c).

children.heading: Notification to licensees
children.label: e Notification to licensees
children.text: Copies of each notice required to be served on a management official under this section shall also be served upon the interested licensee.

children.label: A
children.text: held in the Federal judicial district or in the territory in which the principal office of the licensee is located, unless the party afforded the hearing consents to another place; and

children.label: B
children.text: conducted in accordance with the provisions of chapter 5 of title 5.

children.heading: Issuance of orders
children.label: 2 Issuance of orders
children.text: After a hearing provided for in this section, and not later than 90 days after the Administrator has notified the parties that the case has been submitted for final decision, the Administrator shall render a decision in the matter (which shall include findings of fact upon which its decision is predicated), and shall issue and cause to be served upon each party to the proceeding an order or orders consistent with the provisions of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: at any time, upon such notice, and in such manner as the Administrator deems proper, unless a petition for review is timely filed in a court of appeals of the United States, as provided in paragraph (4)(B), and thereafter until the record in the proceeding has been filed in accordance with paragraph (4)(C); and

children.label: B
children.text: upon such filing of the record, with permission of the court.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Judicial review of an order issued under this section shall be exclusively as provided in this subsection.

children.heading: Petition for review
children.label: B Petition for review
children.text: Any party to a hearing provided for in this section may obtain a review of any order issued pursuant to paragraph (2) (other than an order issued with the consent of the management official concerned, or an order issued under subsection (d)), by filing in the court of appeals of the United States for the circuit in which the principal office of the licensee is located, or in the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit, not later than 30 days after the date of service of such order, a written petition praying that the order of the Administrator be modified, terminated, or set aside.

children.heading: Notification to administration
children.label: C Notification to administration
children.text: A copy of a petition filed under subparagraph (B) shall be forthwith transmitted by the clerk of the court to the Administrator, and thereupon the Administrator shall file in the court the record in the proceeding, as provided in .section 2112 of title 28

children.label: i
children.text: the court shall have jurisdiction, which, upon the filing of the record under subparagraph (C), shall be exclusive, to affirm, modify, terminate, or set aside, in whole or in part, the order of the Administrator, except as provided in the last sentence of paragraph (3)(B);

children.label: ii
children.text: review of such proceedings shall be had as provided in chapter 7 of title 5; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the judgment and decree of the court shall be final, except that the judgment and decree shall be subject to review by the Supreme Court of the United States upon certiorari, as provided in .section 1254 of title 28

children.heading: Judicial review not a stay
children.label: E Judicial review not a stay
children.text: The commencement of proceedings for judicial review under this paragraph shall not, unless specifically ordered by the court, operate as a stay of any order issued by the Administrator under this section.
